title: Mikania pohlii
taxonomy: Mikania pohlii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This plant is a member of the tribe Eupatorieae, which is a group of plants characterized by their composite flowers. The exact classification of Mikania pohlii may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a species within the Asteraceae family.

intro: Mikania pohlii, also known as Mikania, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Mikania pohlii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Mikania pohlii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce pain and inflammation, and support immune function.
